name: Comet ML
what_it_does: ML experiment tracking and LLM observability platform that logs training metrics, compares experiments, manages model versions, and monitors production LLM applications via a REST API and Python SDK.
use_cases: Logging ML training runs with metrics, parameters, and artifacts for experiment comparison
Managing model versions and deployment tracking in the Comet model registry
Monitoring LLM application quality and costs in production via Comet Opik
Querying experiment results via API for automated model selection pipelines
Collaborative ML experiment management across data science teams
not_for: Production infrastructure monitoring (use Datadog or Prometheus for ops metrics)
Teams with very simple ML workflows not needing experiment comparison
Organizations requiring on-premise ML tracking without any SaaS component
best_when: Your team trains ML models and needs experiment tracking with LLM monitoring in a single platform, especially if you want an alternative to Weights & Biases.
avoid_when: You're already deeply invested in W&B or MLflow, or your ML workflows are simple enough that local logging suffices.

auth.notes: API keys are user-scoped and set via COMET_API_KEY environment variable. No fine-grained per-project permission scoping per key. Keys created in Comet dashboard.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: up to 3 users, limited experiment retention
pricing.notes: Free tier is useful for individual ML practitioners. Team pricing requires contacting sales. Comet Opik (LLM monitoring) has its own free tier.

performance.rate_limits: Not prominently documented; SDK handles batching

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API returns standard HTTP codes with JSON error details. SDK provides more descriptive errors than raw API calls.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Experiment creation generates new experiments each time. Metric logging is cumulative, not idempotent. Model registry versioning is idempotent by version number.
known_agent_gotchas: Experiment (run) IDs are auto-generated — store the experiment key after creation for future API calls
Metric logging is append-only — agents should not re-log the same step metrics as it creates duplicates in charts
Comet and Comet Opik (LLM product) use different SDK initialization patterns — verify which product you're integrating
Large artifact uploads (models, datasets) should use the artifact API, not the experiment log_asset — different size limits
REST API token format differs from SDK init — check documentation for the correct authentication header format
